title: Riedelia decurva
taxonomy: Riedelia decurva belongs to the family Zingiberaceae. This family comprises various plant species commonly known as the ginger family. Riedelia decurva is a species of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of the world. It is a member of the genus Riedelia, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The classification of Riedelia decurva further categorizes it as a monocotyledonous plant, characterized by having one cotyledon in its seed.

intro: Riedelia decurva is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throat, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Riedelia decurva include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
